page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-128 is a general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 128 vCPUs mapped to 64 physical cores with multithreading enabled, delivering a dedicated CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 512 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is not bundled with this instance, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, scoring in the top 10% on stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU tests, including physics, encryption, and extended instructions. Cached memory bandwidth is also in the top 10%, reaching 6959.33 GB/sec for small blocks, though uncached large-block read and write speeds fall into the average range. Single-core performance is strong, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests. It also achieves strong results in software compilation, ray tracing, and image processing.

This server is highly suited for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM prompt processing. Its high resource density and dedicated AMD EPYC cores offer qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy parallel tasks that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-128 is a general-purpose server configured with 128 vCPUs and 512 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance allocates 64 physical cores with multithreading and provides a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance, placing in the top 10% for PassMark and stress-ng multi-core tests, alongside strong memory bandwidth for cached operations. The instance is highly effective for parallelized workloads, including software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and prompt processing for medium-sized language models. It offers a resource-dense, cost-efficient profile for enterprise-scale applications requiring high CPU capacity without dedicated GPU acceleration.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-128 is a general-purpose server featuring 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-128 features 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and AMD EPYC processors, optimized for multi-core enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density, multi-core x86_64 server with balanced memory configuration for demanding parallel processing and enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 512 GB RAM powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 processors
Top-tier multi-core CPU and encryption performance in benchmarks
Balanced 4.0 GB memory-to-core ratio with high cached read bandwidth
No local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included
Optimized for software compilation, database operations, and web serving
